steps.title: Identify the central action
steps.content: Leo has “begged Papá [his father] to take him to see La Reina” for as long as he can remember, imagines being a hero if he visits first, and hopes “this summer Papá would finally take him.” Every detail in the passage centers on Leo’s intense desire to visit La Reina.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• "To portray how proud Leo's father is of Leo": Papá’s feelings toward Leo are never described. The text only notes Papá doesn’t believe in ghosts. Violated rule: unsupported inference not grounded in the text. ✗
  • "To explain why Leo doesn't want to grow up yet": The detail that Leo is “almost twelve” suggests anticipation of growing up, not reluctance. The passage contradicts this interpretation. ✗
  • "To criticize Leo for disliking summer": Leo hopes “this summer” Papá will take him, indicating he looks forward to summer. The passage does not suggest Leo dislikes it. ✗
  • "To show how much Leo wants to visit La Reina": The repeated begging, the hero fantasy, and the hope that Papá will “finally” take him all converge on this single purpose. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
